content: [Image #1]  I keep seeing this message when I start a new cloud session.

Mon: May 11, 2026 10:15 PDT — GSD-T v3.26.11 — CURRENT

That warning fires when `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` exceeds 40k chars — yours is 43.5k. It's loaded into every session's context, so the size adds latency and burns tokens on every turn before you've done anything.
will 3 cause any change in functionality.

Mon: May 11, 2026 10:16 PDT — GSD-T v3.26.11 — CURRENT

No — if done correctly. The content stays available; it just stops auto-loading every turn.

**What changes functionally:**
- Install-time blocks (hook JSON configs, setup recipes) — only needed when *setting up* hooks, not on every prompt. Moving them to `~/.claude/setup-notes.md` means I won't see them by default, but you'd reference that file when actually configuring things.
- Same for rarely-triggered reference material.

**What stays in `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`:**
- Prime Directives, Destructive Action Guard, Pre-Commit Gate, Live Clock Rule, Auto-Route, Autonomy Levels, banner format — anything that shapes *every turn's behavior*.

**The risk:** if I move something that's actually load-bearing per-turn (e.g., a rule I need to follow without being asked), behavior degrades silently. Mitigation: move only content that's clearly install/setup/reference, not behavioral rules.

**Honest take:** #1 (pure dedup) has zero risk — the project CLAUDE.md already enforces those rules in this repo. #3 has small but real risk unless we're careful about what moves. If you want #3, I'd do it as: #1 first, measure, then move only the obvious install blocks (hook JSON) — leave all behavioral rules alone.
